designs.title: The Awkward Weeper
designs.excerpt: Three words arrived like a note slipped under a cinema door — weeper, awkwardly, castorin — and a late-night arthouse theatre condensed around them: amber wax, film grain, and the grace of crying in public.

designs.title: The Ponent Survey
designs.excerpt: Three words arrive like field notes — cholanic, ponent, unprobed — and a 19th-century expedition condenses around them: bile chemistry charted against the amber light of a western horizon that was never fully mapped.

designs.title: The Subfusc Bathhouse
designs.excerpt: Three words arrive like steam through a keyhole — tritopine, subfusc, tubbable — and a bathhouse condenses around them, held in permanent amber dusk.

designs.title: The Derisory Club
designs.excerpt: A mock-luxury members' society rooted in tropical swamp and laughable dues — where the nutria outranks everyone, the balibago provides shade for nothing, and membership confers no meaningful advantage whatsoever.

designs.title: Diseased Eden
designs.excerpt: Three words arrived like spores — pyorrheic, cowbind, horsing — and a garden grew from them that was never meant to stop.

designs.title: Primary Venditation
designs.excerpt: The obolus — smallest coin of the ancient world — meets the primal act of selling: a broadsheet from the agora, copper-warm, verdigris-edged, built on three words that collide like exchange itself.

designs.title: Pentite Harvest
designs.excerpt: Five-sided crystal geometry collides with pale gold sediment — a mineral specimen gallery suspended between geological time and harvest light.

designs.title: Arterial Slumber
designs.excerpt: Three words arrived at dusk — half-asleep, unbrushed, still warm with something left unmapped inside the body.

designs.title: Playerdom Apothecary
designs.excerpt: A neon-acid underground kingdom where aceology meets the skunktop — remedies for players who never fold, dispensed in glitch and glow.

designs.title: Construction of Ruins
designs.excerpt: Scaffolding rises from mounded limestone. Bryozoan colonies lace the stone like fossilized blueprints. We build around what we cannot understand, and call it architecture.

designs.title: Botanical Transmission
designs.excerpt: A Victorian naturalist's field journal meets cellular biology — watercolour-washed, parchment-warm, built from three impossible words.

designs.title: Before the Silence
designs.excerpt: Hinoki, stone, and the space before words. A meditative Japanese-inspired design built from three unexpected words and warm cedar light.

designs.title: Liquid Chrome
designs.excerpt: A surface that reflects everything and remembers nothing. Metallic, minimal, in motion.
